howItWorks.intro: This roth ira calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: All growth is tax-free in a Roth IRA
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is a Roth IRA?
faq.answer: A retirement account where you contribute after-tax dollars and all growth and withdrawals in retirement are completely tax-free. No required minimum distributions during your lifetime.

faq.question: What is the 2026 contribution limit?
faq.answer: $7,000 per year ($8,000 if age 50+). Income limits apply: single filers phase out at $150,000-$165,000 MAGI, married at $236,000-$246,000.

faq.question: Roth IRA vs traditional IRA?
faq.answer: Roth: no tax deduction now, tax-free withdrawals later. Traditional: tax deduction now, taxed withdrawals later. Roth wins if your tax rate will be higher in retirement.

faq.question: Can I withdraw contributions early?
faq.answer: Yes, you can withdraw your contributions (not earnings) at any time without penalty or taxes. This makes Roth IRAs more flexible than other retirement accounts.

faq.question: Is the Roth IRA Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Roth IRA Calculator?
sections.body: The Roth IRA Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your current balance, annual contribution, annual return, you get instant results including tax-free balance, total contributions, tax-free growth. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting tax-free balance right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current balance and need to find the right tax-free balance. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Balance ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current balance — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: annual contribution, annual return, years to grow. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your tax-free balance, total contributions, tax-free growth. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current balance before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both tax-free balance and total contributions — they work together to give you the full picture.
